info.title: DotAAS Part 2 | HTTP/REST | Asset Administration Shell Repository Service Specification
info.description:
The Full Profile of the Asset Administration Shell Repository Service Specification as part of the [Specification of the Asset Administration Shell: Part 2](https://industrialdigitaltwin.org/en/content-hub/aasspecifications).  
Copyright: Industrial Digital Twin Association (IDTA) 2025

get.summary: Returns all Asset Administration Shells

post.summary: Creates a new Asset Administration Shell

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

get.summary: Returns References to all Asset Administration Shells

200.description: Requested Asset Administration Shells as a list of References

get.summary: Returns a specific Asset Administration Shell

put.summary: Creates or updates an existing Asset Administration Shell

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

default.description: Default error handling for unmentioned status codes

get.summary: Returns a specific Asset Administration Shell as a Reference
Returns the Asset Information

200.description: The thumbnail of the Asset Information.
Thumbnail deletion successful.  Deprecated: Use response code 204 instead.

get.summary: Returns all submodel references

post.summary: Creates a submodel reference at the Asset Administration Shell

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

delete.summary: Deletes the submodel reference from the Asset Administration Shell. Does not delete the submodel itself!

get.summary: Returns the Submodel

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Determines the structural depth of the respective resource content

delete.summary: Deletes the submodel from the Asset Administration Shell and the Repository.

get.summary: Returns the Submodel's metadata elements

patch.summary: Updates the metadata attributes of the Submodel

get.summary: Returns the Submodel's ValueOnly representation

patch.summary: Updates the values of the Submodel

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Determines the structural depth of the respective resource content

requestBody.description: Submodel object in the ValueOnly representation

get.summary: Returns the Submodel as a Reference
Returns the elements of this submodel in path notation.
Returns all submodel elements including their hierarchy

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

get.summary: Returns all submodel elements including their hierarchy
Returns all submodel elements including their hierarchy in the ValueOnly representation

200.description: List of found submodel elements in their ValueOnly representation

get.summary: Returns all submodel elements as a list of References

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Determines the structural depth of the respective resource content

200.description: List of References of the found submodel elements

get.summary: Returns all submodel elements including their hierarchy

200.description: List of found submodel elements in the Path notation

get.summary: Returns a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path

post.summary: Creates a new submodel element at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

put.summary: Creates or updates an existing submodel element at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

Location.description: URL of the newly created resource

patch.summary: Updates an existing submodel element value at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

requestBody.description: The updated value of the submodel element

delete.summary: Deletes a submodel element at a specified path within the submodel elements hierarchy

get.summary: Returns the metadata attributes if a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path

patch.summary: Updates the metadata attributes of an existing submodel element value at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

requestBody.description: The updated metadata attributes of the submodel element

get.summary: Returns a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path in the ValueOnly representation

200.description: Requested submodel element in its ValueOnly representation

patch.summary: Updates the value of an existing submodel element value at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

requestBody.description: The updated value of the submodel element

get.summary: Returns the Reference of a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Determines the structural depth of the respective resource content

200.description: Requested submodel element in its ValueOnly representation

get.summary: Returns a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path in the Path notation

200.description: Requested submodel element in the Path notation

get.summary: Downloads file content from a specific submodel element from the Submodel at a specified path

Content-Disposition.description: In order to physically download the file usually set to attachment with a filename

put.summary: Uploads file content to an existing submodel element at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

delete.summary: Deletes file content of an existing submodel element at a specified path within submodel elements hierarchy

post.summary: Synchronously invokes an Operation at a specified path
Synchronously invokes an Operation at a specified path
Asynchronously invokes an Operation at a specified path
Asynchronously invokes an Operation at a specified path

get.summary: Returns the Operation status of an asynchronous invoked Operation

200.description: Operation result object containing information that the 'executionState' is still 'Running'

get.summary: Returns the Operation result of an asynchronous invoked Operation
Returns the ValueOnly notation of the Operation result of an asynchronous invoked Operation
Returns an appropriate serialization based on the specified format (see SerializationFormat)

parameters.name: aasIds
parameters.description: The Asset Administration Shells' unique ids (UTF8-BASE64-URL-encoded)

get.summary: Returns the self-describing information of a network resource (ServiceDescription)

AssetIds.name: assetIds
AssetIds.description: A list of specific Asset identifiers. Each Asset identifier is a base64-url-encoded [SpecificAssetId](https://api.swaggerhub.com/domains/Plattform_i40/Part1-MetaModel-Schemas/V3.1.1#/components/schemas/SpecificAssetId)

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: The maximum number of elements in the response array

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: A server-generated identifier retrieved from pagingMetadata that specifies from which position the result listing should continue

AssetAdministrationShellIdentifier.name: aasIdentifier
AssetAdministrationShellIdentifier.description: The Asset Administration Shell’s unique id (UTF8-BASE64-URL-encoded)

Level.name: level
Level.description: Determines the structural depth of the respective resource content

Extent.name: extent
Extent.description: Determines to which extent the resource is being serialized

IdShortPath.name: idShortPath
IdShortPath.description: IdShort path to the submodel element (dot-separated)

HandleId.name: handleId
HandleId.description: The returned handle id of an operation’s asynchronous invocation used to request the current state of the operation’s execution (UTF8-BASE64-URL-encoded)

ValueOnly.description: The ValueOnly serialization (patternProperties and propertyNames will probably be supported with OpenApi 3.1). For the full description of the generic JSON validation schema see the ValueOnly-Serialization as defined in the 'Specification of the Asset Administration Shell - Part 2'.

result.description: Since patternProperties and propertyNames are not supported by OpenApi yet, the ValueOnly serialization for this elements works with the key-attribute as the JSON-property name and the value-attribute as the corresponding value.

MultiLanguagePropertyValue.description: Since patternProperties and propertyNames are not supported by OpenApi yet, the ValueOnly serialization for this elements works with the key-attribute as the JSON-property name and the value-attribute as the corresponding value.

SpecificAssetIdValue.description: Since patternProperties and propertyNames are not supported by OpenApi yet, the ValueOnly serialization for this elements works with the key-attribute as the JSON-property name and the value-attribute as the corresponding value.

ServiceDescription.description: The Description object enables servers to present their capabilities to the clients, in particular which profiles they implement. At least one defined profile is required. Additional, proprietary attributes might be included. Nevertheless, the server must not expect that a regular client understands them.

default.description: Default error handling for unmentioned status codes

bad-request.description: Bad Request, e.g. the request parameters of the format of the request body is wrong.

unauthorized.description: Unauthorized, e.g. the server refused the authorization attempt.

conflict.description: Conflict, a resource which shall be created exists already. Might be thrown if an object with the same id (for Identifiables) or idShort (for Referables within the same Container Element or Submodel) is contained in a POST request.

no-content.description: No Content, request was successful but no content is returned

accepted.description: The server has accepted the request.

Location.description: The URL where the client can find more information about the request.

found.description: The target resource is available but at a different location.

Location.description: The URL where the client can find the target resource.
